theme: one questioning strategy versus differently-shuffled haystacks
title: One Question, Four Shuffled Haystacks
strategies: trivial: ignore the pools, scan items 0,1,2,... one at a time (no adaptivity, ~0.1)

greedy: pool the 4 priors into one mixture distribution and build a textbook weighted binary-search tree over items in id order, always cutting the current contiguous range as close to half the mixture mass as possible -- optimal for the AVERAGE pool, but a cut can straddle a single pool's own clumped cluster and cost that pool extra probes

strong: infer each pool's home cluster from the given weights, then build the tree top-down over WHOLE clusters only -- at each step exhaustively choose which still-undetermined clusters to bucket into the probe so the pooled mass split is as even as possible while never slicing through a cluster, so no individual scenario's clumping is ever wastefully straddled; only within a single resolved cluster does it fall back to a plain weighted binary search
invalid: guesses item 0 for every target without ever probing -- wrong almost everywhere, scores 0.0
